t_ch: 地底下那么大一块，我只是带走一小颗唉。
t_en: They've got loads of it down there. I just chipped off a tiny piece.

t_ch: 我能理解少女对发光宝石的热爱……
t_en: I can understand a young girl's fondness for glittering gemstones...

t_ch: 一杯仙人快乐茶…
t_en: A cup of Immortal's Delight...

t_ch: 这是我对仙舟的美好回忆啦！
t_en: One of my fondest memories on the Xianzhou!

t_ch: 我什么也没说啊。哼…我还是比较喜欢星芋啵啵。
t_en: Hmph, no comment... I happen to prefer startaro bubble tea.

t_ch: 这些应该够了…
t_en: That should be enough...

t_ch: 只有这些吗？三月小姐能不能拿出失去记忆之前就拥有的东西。
t_en: Is that it? Miss March, are you not able to provide possessions from before you lost your memory?

t_ch: 等等，既然记不得，又怎么知道这件东西是不是在失去记忆前获得的？
t_en: Before I lost my memory... So, something I don't remember getting? Even then, I couldn't be sure it was from before I lost my memory, right?

t_ch: 我知道这句话听起来也许有些怪。但推演过去，正需要那时相关的材料，三月小姐不妨再回忆回忆？
t_en: It may be strange to you, but to infer the past, I need materials that were pertinent at that time. Could you try recalling some memories again?

t_ch: 失忆之前，也就是登上列车以前吗……
t_en: From before the memory loss, before the Express...

t_ch: 对不住啦太卜，姬子姐说，拣到我的时候我被赤条条地封在了一块冰里——就像刚出生的婴儿一样，身边没有任何东西。
t_en: I'm sorry, Master Diviner! When Himeko found me in the ice, I was stark naked — like a newborn baby. I didn't have any items with me...

t_ch: 你下一句一定会问，「那块冰还留着吗？」
t_en: ...and before you ask, no, I don't have any of that ice lying around.

t_ch: 很遗憾，在我被打捞上来不久，那块冰就化得不见踪影了。
t_en: It melted away not long after I was rescued.

t_ch: 三月，那可不见得哦？
t_en: ...You sure about that, March?

t_ch: 你不是还能凝结六相冰吗？
t_en: ...What about your Six-Phased Ice?

t_ch: 对呀！{NICKNAME}，你脑袋可真灵光呀。 说到我失忆前就拥有的，应该就是凝出「六相冰」的能力吧？太卜，我随手变出的这块冰，应该能符合要求吧？
t_en: Oh yeah! {NICKNAME}, you sure are a smart cookie. My Six-Phased Ice ability must date from before the memory loss. Master Diviner, any ice I create should do, right?

t_ch: 这样也好。我着人将东西送往穷观阵的阵基处。三月小姐，准备好了就站到阵心去。推演随时可以开始。
t_en: It should indeed, I'll have someone place the material in the base terminal of the Matrix. Miss March, please position yourself in the Matrix core — we may begin our inference whenever you are ready.

t_ch: 片刻之后，阵基开合，符箓生光，穷观阵已准备完毕。
t_en: Moments later, the apparatuses of the Matrix come to life with clicks and whirrs. It is time.

t_ch: 阵法已经准备就绪了。三月小姐，你准备好了吗？
t_en: The Matrix has cleared diagnostics. Miss March, are you ready yet?

t_ch: 呃，我大概应该可能已经准备好了……
t_en: Uhh, I'm pretty sure I should be, almost k—kinda ready to go...

t_ch: 务必请坚定信心。不是所有的回忆都适合被唤醒的。一路走来，人们也承受了许多痛苦和负担。
t_en: Please be prepared. Not all memories are suitable for reawakening. People carry with them much pain and suffering.

t_ch: 如果你没有做好准备，我们可以暂停推演。本座万万不希望自己好心办了坏事，反而使你受到伤害。
t_en: If you're not ready, we can pause the inference process. I would never wish for good intentions to result in harm.

t_ch: 没、没关系的！来都来了。是骡子是马，都得当活的医！
t_en: I—I'm okay! Let's do it! Only way to find out is to give it a go!

t_ch: 那么，接下来，本座会将你连接到大衍穷观阵上，而穷观阵会读取你的记忆，引导你回溯自己的过往。
t_en: Very well. I will soon connect you to the Matrix of Prescience Ultima, where the Matrix will read your memories and guide you back into your past.

t_ch: 如果回溯到了那个阻塞你回忆的关隘，穷观阵就会利用已经获得的信息进行推演，构建出你可能存在的过去。
t_en: If you reach a block in your memory, the Matrix will use the data gathered to derive a scenario, and construct a possible past for you.

t_ch: 我了解了…{NICKNAME}也会跟咱一起吗？
t_en: Got it... Oh, uh, can {NICKNAME} join me, too?

t_ch: 抱歉，你没办法带上{F#她}{M#他}。你是穷观阵所观测演算的中枢，{NICKNAME}的加入只会带来冗余的情报。
t_en: I'm sorry, that would be highly inadvisable. You form the nexus of the Matrix's inference — {NICKNAME} would only introduce redundant data to the system.

t_ch: 换句话说，在穷观阵推演所构筑的记忆宫殿里，能依靠的只有你自己。
t_en: In other words, inside the memory palace that the Matrix constructs, the only person you'll be able to count on is yourself.

t_ch: 加油，三月！
t_en: You can do it, March!

t_ch: 我放心不下。
t_en: ...Can't say I'm filled with confidence.

t_ch: 没事啦，不用那么担心！我已经不是小孩子啦！
t_en: It's cool, I'll be fine! I'm not a little kid anymore!

t_ch: 别太担心，本座在穷观阵外有能力与你随时沟通，可以有限度地干涉回溯进程。
t_en: Don't worry. I can communicate with you from outside the Matrix and manipulate the recollection process to a certain extent.

t_ch: 开始吧。
t_en: Let's begin.

t_ch: 我们可以继续了吗？
t_en: May we proceed?

t_ch: 继续吧。
t_en: Yes.

t_ch: 再等等。
t_en: Wait.

t_ch: 符玄小姐？是你吗？
t_en: Miss Fu Xuan? Is that you?

t_ch: 你找错人了，去和另一位聊聊。
t_en: Wrong Fu Xuan! Talk to the other one.

t_ch: 符玄小姐？是你吗？
t_en: Miss Fu Xuan? Is that you?

t_ch: 若要解决星核灾变，首先便要厘清一个问题：问题的关键究竟为何? 我们一般认为，抓住了问题的关键，其他一切就能迎刃而解。
t_en: If we want to resolve the Stellaron crisis, we must refine the question: What is the crux of the problem? Generally, it is believed that when one seizes on the crux of a problem, the problem will split open like bamboo on the edge of a knife.

t_ch: 星核催生建木带来的后果将是所有人无法预料的。古人云福兮祸所伏，祸兮福所倚。天道有常，在福祸无可占卜的当下，唯有神通广大的本座才能救罗浮于水火！
t_en: Nobody could have foreseen the catalyzing effect of the Stellaron on the Ambrosial Arbor. As the ancients said, blessings bring about misfortune, and vice versa. Nature has its own algorithm, and it is precisely when fate wends betwixt blessing and misfortune that I, a master diviner of masterful proportions, am called on to save the people of the Luofu!

t_ch: 她这长篇大论的都在说个什么劲啊？太卜你当时说过这些话吗，我怎么不记得了？
t_en: What the heck is she talking about? Master Diviner, did you actually say that at the time? I can't remember.

t_ch: 看来在你的心目中，本座是个只会长篇大论，净说些废话的人。
t_en: It would appear that in Miss March's estimation, I'm somewhat of a... blabbermouth.

t_ch: 怎、怎么会呢！我心目中的太卜，那可是神通广大啊！嘿嘿。
t_en: N—No! Uh — I've always seen you as a... m—masterful master diviner! Hehe...

t_ch: 虽然这段经历已明显失真了，但构成它的素材却是取自于你的记忆，不会骗人的。也就是说，在你的潜意识中，认为本座说的那些话…和绕口令差不多。
t_en: Despite the obvious distortions in this experience, the base materials from which it is composed are taken from your actual memory — there is no falsehood here. In other words, your subconscious mind thinks that I... talk in tongue-twisters.

t_ch: 哈哈哈…主要是，平时听太卜的高论，确实听不太懂嘛……
t_en: Hahaha... Uh, — it's mainly just that I, uh... can't always comprehend the... lofty ideals and, uh... principles you discuss...

t_ch: 罢了，眼下不是置气的时候。
t_en: Forget it, now is not the time for losing one's temper.

t_ch: 推演的经历失真，咱们就集中注意力把「失真」的因素一个个挑出来，加以清除纠正，不然也没法进一步回溯你的过去。
t_en: If the inferred experience is distorted, we should focus on filtering out each distortion one by one — otherwise we won't be able to trace your memories back any further.

t_ch: 好复杂，听得我都要长脑子了…有没有更简单的解释？
t_en: Umm, this is getting a little complicated... Is there a... simpler explanation?

t_ch: 来，张弓搭箭，射向那个符玄。
t_en: *sigh* Draw your bow and fire an arrow at that Fu Xuan.

t_ch: 真是吓死了，突然就开始打架了…我看看，这里有停云小姐、杨叔、景元将军…还有{NICKNAME}……
t_en: That freaked me out — I don't like people suddenly deciding to fight me... Now, let's see who's here. Tingyun, Welt, General Jing Yuan... and also {NICKNAME}...

t_ch: 我不会要挨个和他们打一架吧…太卜大人倒是还好，景元将军我大概真的打不过……
t_en: I don't have to fight all of them... right? The Master Diviner was easy-peasy, but I don't think I could take General Jing Yuan...

t_ch: 什么叫「太卜大人倒是还好」？
t_en: "Easy-peasy"!?

t_ch: 令此处失真的「干涉因素」应该只有一个源头。也就是那个「此刻不应在场但却在场」的人。
t_en: Hmph... The interference factor creating the distortion here should only have one source. That is, the person present who shouldn't be.

t_ch: 所以，我要从这些人里挑一个出来，清除它的「干涉」？
t_en: So, I have to pick someone and clear them of "interference"?

t_ch: 正是如此。
t_en: Exactly.

t_ch: 为了防止干扰你的判断…本座暂时先撤去投影。你能听到我的声音，就代表我在看护着你。
t_en: My projection will disappear for the moment so as not to cloud your judgment... If you can hear my voice, then it means I'm watching over you.

t_ch: 太卜大人真可靠！
t_en: You're the best, Master Diviner!

t_ch: 哼哼，希望这份可靠能取代我在你心里那个著名废话表演艺术家的光辉形象～
t_en: Hmph, I hope the "best" version of me can overwrite the gloriously absurd version that lives in your subconsciousness.

t_ch: 三月小姐，要不要抽一发最新推出的「仙舟美人图鉴」呢？这段时间抽到限定卡「罗浮俏郎君」的概率会大大提升哦～
t_en: Roll up, roll up! Miss March, would you like to take a look at the latest edition of the Xianzhou Beauty Index? The chance of drawing a Luofu Playboy card is suuuuper high right now~

t_ch: 看到这么有精神的停云小姐，忽然觉得有些伤感。
t_en: Miss Tingyun... It's great to see you so happy... but I can't help feeling a little sad.

t_ch: 过去的事情都过去了，伤感什么！今朝有酒今朝醉，明日愁来明日愁嘛。不许伤感了～
t_en: The past is in the past! What is there to feel sad about? Live every day as if it were your last. Don't be sad~

t_ch: （清除「失真」）
t_en: (Clear distortion)

t_ch: （再等等）
t_en: (Wait)

t_ch: 哟！{NICKNAME}！这么巧，你也在我记忆里呀。
t_en: Hey, {NICKNAME}! Fancy seeing you here.

t_ch: 你平时不爱说话也就算了，这可是我的记忆，我的地盘！你好歹得给我说句话！
t_en: Hey, it's fine if you don't want to talk in real life, but this is MY memory, so it's MY rules! Come on, say something!

t_ch: 句话。
t_en: Something.

t_ch: …给我整无语了。
t_en: ...Seriously?

t_ch: 咱们两个印象中的{NICKNAME}倒是差不多。
t_en: We have similar recollections of {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 不要回忆，这于你无益。
t_en: Leave your memories in the past — they have no place in the present.

t_ch: 太卜大人听见了吗，{F#她}{M#他}怎么也突然开始说些奇怪的话……
t_en: Did you hear that, Master Diviner? Now {F#she's}{M#he's} talking weird...

t_ch: （清除「失真」）
t_en: (Clear distortion)

t_ch: （再等等）
t_en: (Wait)

t_ch: 杨叔！你好呀！
t_en: Mr. Yang! Hi there!

t_ch: 小三月，做好准备，接下来的战斗会非常危险。
t_en: March, get ready. The next fight is going to be very dangerous.

t_ch: 嗯？接下来的战斗？
t_en: Huh? Which next fight?

t_ch: 清醒点，别发梦了。幻胧朝着建木的方向去了，如果不去阻止绝灭大君，必将会有一场浩劫。
t_en: Stay focused. Phantylia is headed for the Ambrosial Arbor — if we don't stop the Lord Ravager, it'll be a catastrophe.

t_ch: 好的好的，我知道啦。
t_en: Okay, alright. Got it.

t_ch: 此外…小三月，未来对我们来说才是最重要的，不是吗？
t_en: Another thing... The future is what's most important for us. Don't you agree, March?

t_ch: 什么？
t_en: What?

t_ch: 沉湎于回忆，会麻痹你对当下的感受，也会阻止你对未来的希望。
t_en: Dwelling on memories can numb your sense of the present, and inhibit future ambitions.

t_ch: 符玄小姐，记忆里的人都会说这种莫名其妙的话吗？
t_en: Miss Fu Xuan, do people in memories always say weird stuff like this?

t_ch: 这失真的情况有些古怪……
t_en: The distortions we're experiencing are... unorthodox.

t_ch: （清除「失真」）
t_en: (Clear distortion)

t_ch: （再等等）
t_en: (Wait)

t_ch: 将军大人，你好呀！
t_en: General, hello!

t_ch: 将军大人？
t_en: General?

t_ch: 景元将军？
t_en: General Jing Yuan?

t_ch: 景元！！！
t_en: Jing Yuan!!!

t_ch: 好生气啊！竟然站在这里睡着了！天底下怎么会有这种人啊！
t_en: Ugh, what's his deal? Who falls asleep standing up anyway!?

t_ch: 这分明就是你潜意识里对将军的偏见……
t_en: This is clearly your subconscious projecting onto the general...

t_ch: 我清楚记得，咱们攻入丹鼎司时，将军是不在现场的。看起来，他就是本段经历中失真干扰的源头。
t_en: Ah! I remember now! The general wasn't there when we broke into the Alchemy Commission. Looks like we've found the source of our distortion.

t_ch: （清除「失真」）
t_en: (Clear distortion)

t_ch: 三月七。
t_en: March 7th.

t_ch: 吓我一跳…你怎么突然醒了？
t_en: Ah!!! You scared me... Why'd you wake up so suddenly?

t_ch: 你不满足于自己当下的生活吗？
t_en: Are you not satisfied with your life as it is?

t_ch: 我…我还挺满足的……
t_en: I... I'm satisfied...

t_ch: 那又何必执着于回望过去呢？
t_en: Then why are you obsessing over the past?

t_ch: （再等等…）
t_en: (Wait)

t_ch: 嗯？这个突然出现的东西是什么？
t_en: Hm? What is that?

t_ch: 这个我知道，这个是贝洛伯格的加热器！嗯…等一下，贝洛伯格的加热器为什么会出现在行医集市？
t_en: I know what this is! It's a Belobog heater! Umm... Wait a minute, what's a Belobog heater doing at the Healer's Market?

t_ch: 看来是一段经历中混进了另一段经历的某个组成部分。就像是侵入的「异物」？
t_en: Looks like part of one experience has infiltrated another. A foreign object, so to speak.

t_ch: 「异物」…听起来是个很糟糕的东西。该不会需要手术摘除吧？
t_en: "Foreign object"? ...That sounds like a medical issue. Will it require surgery?

t_ch: 那倒不必。恰恰相反，我觉得「异物」应该是穷观阵摒除干扰的征兆。它成功回溯了你的另一场经历。
t_en: No. I believe the object is a sign of the Matrix purging distortion. It has managed to trace back to another one of your experiences.

t_ch: 敲打敲打它，看看咱们能不能跳进那段经历。
t_en: Make contact with the object — let's see if we can jump over into that experience.

t_ch: 贝洛伯格…我似乎在星图档案中翻到过这个名字。
t_en: Belobog... I've seen that name somewhere in the astral charts.

t_ch: 仙舟的贸易航路上有许多类似的星球…可惜，反物质军团出现后，它们都失去了联系。
t_en: There are many similar star systems on the Xianzhou trade routes... Unfortunately, all of them lost contact after the appearance of the Antimatter Legion.

t_ch: 是啊，贝洛伯格是一个白雪皑皑的末日孤城…所以到处都是这样的加热器，不然人根本生活不下去。
t_en: Yeah, Belobog is a kind of snowy doomsday city... There are heaters like this all over the place, otherwise people wouldn't survive.

t_ch: 来吧太卜，本小姐带你足不出户见识见识贝洛伯格的风景。
t_en: Come on, Master Diviner — I'll give you a whirlwind tour of Belobog.

t_ch: （触摸「异物」）
t_en: (Touch the "foreign object")

t_ch: …话说，我还从没摸过加热器呢…这玩意儿烧得通红，要是把手放上去，我这细皮嫩肉怕不是会变得金黄酥脆……
t_en: ...Uhh, is touching a heater a good idea? This thing is red-hot — what if my hand turns extra crispy...

t_ch: 没事啦，这是记忆空间。只要你在现实中没被它烫伤过，那在记忆中也自然不会为它所伤……
t_en: It's okay, this is a memory space. If something hasn't injured you in reality, then it cannot injure you in memory...

t_ch: 我在现实中也没挨过景元的打啊……
t_en: Well, I was never beaten up by Jing Yuan in reality, either...

t_ch: 少废话，给我摸。
t_en: Enough of that — touch it.

t_ch: 诶诶诶你别拽我手…烫烫烫烫烫烫！
t_en: Hey! Don't pull on my hand, I — ow, ow, ow, hot, hot, hot!

t_ch: （再等等）
t_en: (Wait)

t_ch: 我的妈呀，可烫死我了！说好的现实中没发生过的事就不会烫到我呢！
t_en: What the actual — that thing was flaming hot! You said things that never happened to me in reality couldn't happen here!

t_ch: 本座说你不会被烫伤，又没说你不会被烫到。只要你能理解「烫」，记忆就会自行补足你的感受。
t_en: I said that if something hasn't injured you in reality, then it cannot injure you in memory..... I didn't say anything about the sensation of injury.

t_ch: 这就是贝洛伯格吗？这地方一点儿也不冷啊？
t_en: So this is Belobog? I was expecting it to be cold.

t_ch: 好冷…唉？太卜你没感觉到吗，我忍不住打哆嗦了。
t_en: It's freezing... Huh? Master Diviner, aren't you cold? I can't stop shivering.

t_ch: 多半是因为本座从没去过真正的苦寒之地。得了，你就自个体会自己经历中的寒冷吧。
t_en: Hmm, most likely a result of me never having experienced true cold. I'm afraid you'll have to do the shivering by yourself.

t_ch: 罗浮那段过去，本座和你一同亲历，也许能帮上忙。但到了贝洛伯格，就只能靠你自己来分辨出「干扰源头」，让「异物」现身了。
t_en: On the Luofu, you and I have shared experiences, and so I was able to provide more active assistance... but in Belobog, you'll have to rely on your own judgment to ascertain the source of distortion and get the foreign object to appear.

t_ch: 别担心，本姑娘已经完全明白这一切了。
t_en: Yeah, yeah, I get it.

t_ch: 对了，三月小姐，还有一件事我得问个明白…你是否有可能经历过什么巨大的痛苦？
t_en: Oh, Miss March, I have one more thing to ask... Have you ever experienced great suffering?

t_ch: 嗯？应该没有吧？为什么突然问这个？
t_en: Huh? I don't think so. Why are you suddenly asking me that?

t_ch: 有时候人为了逃避痛苦，会刻意忘却某些极端的记忆……
t_en: Sometimes people forget extreme memories to protect themselves from suffering...

t_ch: 不知道你注意到没有，在关于罗浮的记忆中，你遇到的每个人都在明里暗里地劝你「不要继续回忆」。
t_en: Perhaps you didn't notice, but in your memories of the Luofu, everyone you met was either hinting or explicitly stating that you should stop trying to remember.

t_ch: 这更像是你的潜意识在对自己说的话。当然也不排除，你的记忆遭到过外力的干涉，谁人在你大脑留下了暗示，避免你回想起什么事。
t_en: I believe this is most likely your subconscious communicating with you directly... though of course, we cannot rule out that external forces may have interfered with your memory. In the latter case, it is possible that someone decided to leave warnings in your subconscious to prevent you from recalling the truth.

t_ch: 我倒是不觉得自己有那么重要啦…还有人会这么大费周章封印我的记忆……
t_en: Am I really that important to someone...? I don't think there's anyone who'd go to all the trouble of locking up my memories...

t_ch: 但你什么都不记得了，不是吗？
t_en: ...And yet, memories are precisely what you seek, no?
Hmm...

t_ch: 所以本座要在这里再向你确认一次——你是否还要继续探索自己的记忆？
t_en: I say this to say one thing — are you certain you wish to continue exploring your memories?

t_ch: 既然你说，可能有外力干扰了我的记忆…那不就更应该探索了嘛！总不能让封印我记忆的坏人逍遥法外吧！走吧，本姑娘带你逛逛这歌德宾馆！
t_en: You said external forces might be messing with my head, right? That just makes me more curious. Hmph, no one gets away with locking up MY memories! Let's go. I'll show you the way to Goethe Hotel!

t_ch: 我对这件事也越来越有兴趣了。
t_en: Hehe, very well. I must admit that I'm finding the whole matter more interesting by the minute.

t_ch: 好久不见啊，三月姐，欢迎来到桑博宾馆！
t_en: Miss March, long time no see! Welcome to the Sampo Hotel!

t_ch: 桑…桑博宾馆？这不是歌德宾馆吗？
t_en: ...Th—The Sampo Hotel? This is Goethe Hotel!

t_ch: 以前是歌德宾馆，现在是桑博宾馆了啊！老歌德年老体衰，无力经营这么大的产业，就把它托付给了自己最信任的年轻人——桑博！
t_en: WAS Goethe Hotel, hehe — now it's the Sampo Hotel! In the autumn of his years, Old Goethe got too old to run this place — so he handed it down to his most trusted young confidant — Sampo Koski!

t_ch: 你可别误会啊，这里面绝不存在任何巧取豪夺，完全是两位绅士之间梦想的承继。
t_en: And before you get the wrong idea, there was ZERO trickery involved — this was a cherished agreement between gentlemen.

t_ch: 你看，我和老歌德都选择了面向未来，而不是死守过去的回忆。你也该这么做的，三月姐。
t_en: You see, Old Goethe and I chose to face the future instead of clinging to memories of the past... you should do the same, March.

t_ch: 这个蓝头发的家伙开口就透出坑蒙拐骗的气息。
t_en: This blue-haired fellow has an air of deceit about him every time he opens his mouth.

t_ch: 哈哈，你真是慧眼识英才。
t_en: Haha, you don't need a jade abacus to get a feel for Sampo...

t_ch: （清除「失真」）
t_en: (Clear distortion)

t_ch: （再等等）
t_en: (Wait)

t_ch: 三月小姐，欢迎光临桑博宾馆，我是这里的保安杰帕德。
t_en: Miss March 7th, welcome to the Sampo Hotel. I'm Gepard, the security here.

t_ch: 这宾馆保安怎么这么全副武装…贝洛伯格的治安很差吗？
t_en: Why would hotel security need to be armed to the teeth? Is there great lawlessness in Belobog?

t_ch: 不不不…杰帕德是银鬃铁卫的戍卫官，就类似罗浮上的云骑队长吧。我也不知道他怎么就成了宾馆的保安了。
t_en: No, no, no... Gepard is a captain of the Silvermane Guards, just like how the Luofu has its own Cloud Knights captains. I have no idea what he's doing running security at a hotel like this though.

t_ch: 不意外。人心总是追求合理，拼拼凑凑便把不相干的东西组成了一出看起来有意义的幻戏故事，取名人生。
t_en: It isn't so surprising. In attempting to pursue the rational, we piece together the unrelated. The result is a seemingly meaningful fantasy — a fantasy called life.

t_ch: 请问三月小姐有什么事吗？没事的话，请不要打扰保安执勤。
t_en: Excuse me, Miss March. Do you need anything? If there's nothing else, please let me get back to my duty.

t_ch: 另外，请允许我提醒您——过去的已经过去了，回想过去毫无意义，您能把握的只有未来。
t_en: Also, allow me to remind you — you cannot change the past. The future is all that's left for you.

t_ch: 怎么说呢，竟然还有点像他会说的话……
t_en: Hmm, that actually does feels like something he would say...

t_ch: （清除「失真」）
t_en: (Clear distortion)

t_ch: （再等等）
t_en: (Wait)

t_ch: 欢迎来到桑博宾馆，三月小姐。
t_en: Welcome to the Sampo Hotel, Miss March.

t_ch: 桑博宾馆曾名为歌德宾馆。后来老歌德年老体衰，桑博利用巧取豪夺的方式霸占了这里，并更名为桑博宾馆。
t_en: The Sampo Hotel used to be called the Goethe Hotel. After Old Goethe retired, Sampo managed to trick his way into acquiring this place, and renamed it after himself.

t_ch: 好在宾馆优质的服务并未受到影响，依然是行政区最好的宾馆。
t_en: Luckily, the high-quality service of the hotel remains undiminished. It's still the best place to stay in the Administrative District.

t_ch: 顺便一提，桑博宾馆的广告词是：「不应回望过去，只应憧憬未来。」我觉得这条广告词也很适合三月小姐。
t_en: I should mention — the Sampo Hotel's new slogan is: "Don't think about the past, strive for your future." Sounds like the kind of ad copy that Miss March would like.

t_ch: 这个女孩子说的话很可信啊。
t_en: This girl seems quite trustworthy.

t_ch: 那是我潜意识里编的吧……
t_en: That's just the work of my subconscious mind...

t_ch: （清除「失真」）
t_en: (Clear distortion)

t_ch: （再等等）
t_en: (Wait)

t_ch: 这歌德宾馆的茶点可是一绝啊…尤其是这鳞渊春…据说有一年，一位筑城者用存护的力量让雪原上开出了山茶花，而这就是用它泡的茶。
t_en: The tea service at Goethe Hotel is first-class... especially this Whale-Tide Spring... They say that an Architect once used the Preservation's power to make camellias bloom on the snow plains, and that this tea is made from those leaves.

t_ch: 「鳞渊春」不是仙舟的茶吗？
t_en: "Whale-Tide Spring" — isn't that a variety of Xianzhou tea?

t_ch: 这大概是我脑补出来的…主要是我也不记得在贝洛伯格喝过什么有名的好茶了。
t_en: My brain must be filling in the blanks... Probably because I never drank any famous tea in Belobog.

t_ch: 苦的，不好喝。
t_en: ...So bitter... yuck.

t_ch: 苦的才对身体好啊！
t_en: Bitter tea is good for your health!

t_ch: 可是苦的就是苦的啊！骗自己吃苦有好处，这不过是一种自我催眠罢了！
t_en: ...Bitter is bitter! If it tastes like that, it doesn't really matter how good for you it is.

t_ch: 贝洛伯格有一句古话，叫做「吃得苦中苦，方为人上人。」
t_en: There's an old saying in Belobog: "Blessed is he who can eat the bitterest of the bitter."

t_ch: 这些经历扭曲得越来越离谱了……
t_en: These experiences are becoming increasingly distorted...

t_ch: 看来我的潜意识没什么编剧天赋啊。
t_en: Seems like my subconscious mind has no talent for screenwriting.

t_ch: 表意识恐怕也没有！
t_en: I'm not sure your conscious mind does either.

t_ch: 无法反驳……
t_en: I'm not denying it...

t_ch: 真是一杯好茶啊…可惜不是每个人都能体会到它的美好。
t_en: What an excellent cup of tea! It's a pity not everyone can appreciate its beauty...

t_ch: 三月小姐，「遗忘」这种恩赐恰如这杯苦茶一样，入口苦涩，却有轻松的余味，可惜品尝它的人总是无法体会它的美好。
t_en: ...much like the gift of forgetting, Miss March. It's bitter at first, but with a gentle aftertaste.

t_ch: 说实话，这个布洛妮娅和我印象中的布洛妮娅…差得很远。
t_en: Hmm, this Bronya is... really different from the Bronya I remember.

t_ch: 扭曲这段经历的力量…很靠近了。
t_en: The force that's distorting this experience... is nearby.

t_ch: 有点瘆得慌……
t_en: Do you have to make it sound all scary...?

t_ch: 安心吧，只要还在穷观阵里，本座就会保障你的安全。
t_en: Rest easy, I can guarantee your safety while you're inside the Matrix of Prescience.

t_ch: （清除「失真」）
t_en: (Clear distortion)

t_ch: （再等等）
t_en: (Wait)

t_ch: 好久不见呀，希儿～
t_en: Long time no see, Seele~

t_ch: 这不是三月七吗？好久不见了啊…你们离开贝洛伯格后，一切还顺利吗？
t_en: March? Long time no see... How's everything been going since you left Belobog?

t_ch: 列车没有回头，列车驶向了星空的另一端…因为这是它的宿命。就像在时间里前进的我们一样，无法倒回过去。
t_en: The Express never looked back, it headed straight for the other end of the galaxy... because that was its fate. The same goes for us — we can't return to the past.

t_ch: 三月七，不要回忆过去，那对你无益有害。请你相信我。
t_en: March, don't dwell on the past, it won't do you any good. Trust me.

t_ch: 呃…知道啦…谢谢你……
t_en: Uh... Got it. Thanks...

t_ch: …怎么你记忆中的每个人都在阻止你回忆…事情变得越来越奇怪了……
t_en: ...These warnings are ongoing... The situation is becoming stranger by the minute.

t_ch: 唉…我也觉得奇怪，但走一步算一步吧！
t_en: It sure is... *sigh* Let's press on and see what awaits.

t_ch: （清除「失真」）
t_en: (Clear distortion)

t_ch: 我们初到贝洛伯格的第二天，银鬃铁卫对咱们几个穷追不舍，然后才遇到了希儿。
t_en: I remember our second day in Belobog was mostly us being hunted down by Silvermane Guards... We didn't meet Seele until way later.

t_ch: 所以希儿不应该出现在这段记忆中。她就是这段记忆中的「干涉源头」。
t_en: Which means... Seele shouldn't be appearing in this memory. She's the source of the interference.

t_ch: 清除干涉吧。
t_en: In which case, we must eliminate her.

t_ch: 看来你不愿意相信我。
t_en: Seems like you don't trust me.

t_ch: 我…我没有不相信你的意思…只是……
t_en: I—It's not that I don't trust you... It's just...

t_ch: 不必回答，不必歉疚。那只是试图阻碍你的一个幻影。
t_en: An answer is not required. She is merely a mirage hindering your progress.

t_ch: 我知道了。那么，开始吧。
t_en: I understand. Let's do this.

t_ch: （再等等）
t_en: (Wait)

t_ch: 这是什么？我瞧瞧…「黑塔空间站自研」？
t_en: What's this? Let me see... "Developed on Herta Space Station"?

t_ch: 看来这就是「异物」了。接下来回溯的经历，应该是我第一次遇见{NICKNAME}的「黑塔」空间站。
t_en: Seems like this must be the foreign object. The next experience should be meeting {NICKNAME} for the first time — Herta Space Station.

t_ch: 只可惜到最后本座也没看到雪国的绝景，而是在一个域外客栈的大堂里待了这么久…看来这地方让你印象很深。
t_en: ...Shame, we didn't get to see the stunning vistas of the snow plains. This hotel lobby must have left a deep impression on you.

t_ch: 在宾馆里美美地睡了一觉，醒来发现自己变成了通缉犯…印象确实是很深。
t_en: Well, waking up after a great sleep in a comfy bed to find out you're a wanted criminal... tends to leave a deep impression.

t_ch: 现在出发去下一站…呃不，上一站了，咱们走吧！
t_en: Let's move on to the next stop... Uh, I mean— the last stop. Let's go!

t_ch: 只要你准备好了就可以出发。
t_en: So long as you're ready.

t_ch: （触摸「异物」）
t_en: (Touch the "foreign object")

t_ch: 太好了，这次只需要摸一台显示器，而不是什么滚烫的加热器……
t_en: Phew, I'll take a monitor over a red-hot heater any day...

t_ch: （再等等）
t_en: (Wait)

t_ch: 三月，清醒一点，我们要抓紧些了。
t_en: March, wake up — we have to hurry.

t_ch: 嗯？出发？符玄小姐呢？
t_en: Huh? Are we going? What about Miss Fu Xuan?

t_ch: 符玄？那是谁？没时间做白日梦了，现在空间站很危险。
t_en: Fu Xuan? Who's that? There's no time for daydreaming — the space station is in crisis.

t_ch: 啊？什么……
t_en: Huh? What do you mean...

t_ch: 跟上我。
t_en: Come with me.

t_ch: 丹恒？人怎么不见了？
t_en: Dan Heng? Where are you?

t_ch: 太卜大人？听得见吗？糟了…一定出了什么麻烦！
t_en: Master Diviner? Can you hear me? Darn it... Something must have gone wrong!

t_ch: 怎么办…符玄小姐不在，突然觉得有点瘆得慌……
t_en: What am I gonna do... Why did Miss Fu Xuan pick the scariest time to vanish...

t_ch: 三月…得见吗……
t_en: March... Can you... me ...

t_ch: 本座现在越来越确信，你的记忆遭到干涉了…只是不知道这干涉究竟从何而来……
t_en: I'm getting more and more evidence that your memories have suffered interference... but I don't know where from...

t_ch: 没办法了…看来这次真的只能靠我自己了。
t_en: *sigh* Looks like I'll have to do it by myself this time.

t_ch: 三月，你不记得自己的经历了吗？你手边那柄长弓便是证据。
t_en: March, have you forgotten your past experiences? Surely your bow there is enough evidence for you.

t_ch: 你曾是一位巡海游侠，你与同伴为了抵抗虫群残余而鏖战，你在爱墨瑞得救援行将被黑洞吞没的世界，你粉碎过原始博士精心罗织的阴谋。
t_en: You were once a Galaxy Ranger engaged in a bitter fighting battle with the remnants of the Swarm. You rescued a planet in the Emerald system that was about to be swallowed by a black hole, smashing through Dr. Primitive's carefully-laid plans.

t_ch: 在翁瓦克的战斗中，你深受重创。为了保护你，义侠之首将你封入寒冰中，待你自行复原。
t_en: You were severely wounded during the battle for Vonwacq. The leader of the Rangers encased you in ice to protect you, and waited for you to recover.

t_ch: 可那柄弓…不是姬子姐给我打造的吗？你的谎言太拙劣啦。
t_en: My bow... But Himeko made that for me! You're lying...

t_ch: （清除「失真」）
t_en: (Clear distortion)

t_ch: （再等等）
t_en: (Wait)

t_ch: 三月七，就算回望，你也只能找到混沌多变的过去。
t_en: March, if you insist on looking back, you'll find nothing but confusion and chaos.

t_ch: 你是宝钻世界亚德丽芬唯一的遗孑，最后的公主。那颗星星在纳努克攀升的瞬间被焚烧成了黑曜的地狱。
t_en: You were the last princess and also the last survivor of the diamond world, Adlivun. The planet was burned to an obsidian hellscape the moment Nanook ascended.

t_ch: 你的父母怀着绝望将襁褓中的你送入寒冰的航船，又怀着希望目睹你没入浩瀚星海的惊涛巨浪中。
t_en: Filled with despair, your parents swaddled you in warm clothing and placed you into an ice ship. As they watched you sink into the turbulent waves of the starscape, they felt a newfound hope.

t_ch: 你被籍籍无名之辈们拣到，抚养。你是唯一的救主，重生的许诺。
t_en: You were found and raised by strangers. You are the only savior — the promise of rebirth.

t_ch: 这…这不是我在小说里给自己编的经历吗？
t_en: Wait... I wrote that! That's a story I made up about my past!

t_ch: 可恶，居然把我的想象都给翻了出来。
t_en: Great, forget memories, now everyone gets to see my imagination...

t_ch: （清除「失真」）
t_en: (Clear distortion)

t_ch: （再等等）
t_en: (Wait)

t_ch: 三月七，星穹列车无名客的一员。
t_en: March 7th, one of the Nameless aboard the Astral Express.

t_ch: 在被姬子拣到后，自封印其身体的六相冰中得到解放。她丧失了自己对过去的回忆。
t_en: After being picked up by Himeko, she was freed from the Six-Phased Ice in which she was encased. She lost all memory of the past...

t_ch: 因为她本就没有过去可言。她曾是一位星神，星神无有过去，当彼等飞升的那刻起，其存在的过去与未来就被命途所淹没。
t_en: ...because she had no past to speak of. She was an Aeon, and Aeons have no past... From the moment they ascend, the past and future of their existence becomes inundated by their Path.

t_ch: 三月七所犯的过错，便是丢失了属于神明的权柄。
t_en: March 7th's mistake was the loss of the authority of the Aeons.

t_ch: 这么离谱的事情…我连想都没想过。不过，你说的很有可能喔！
t_en: Could it be...? That never even occured to me... but I guess it's not impossible!

t_ch: （清除「失真」）
t_en: (Clear distortion)

t_ch: （再等等）
t_en: (Wait)

t_ch: 小三月，不要继续回忆了。
t_en: March, leave your memories in the past.

t_ch: 有什么必要去回想那些过去的事呢？你已将它们遗忘，而这正意味着它们对你并不重要。
t_en: Why do you insist on returning to them? If they were so important to you, wouldn't you have remembered them?

t_ch: 无名客登上列车的那一刻，就意味着重获新生，我们脚下的将是一条永不回头的道路。
t_en: The moment a Nameless boards the Express, a new life begins. We set out on a new road, never to return.

t_ch: 去探索吧，去开拓吧，不要用回头来玷污无名客的荣誉。
t_en: Explore, trailblaze, but do not dwell on the past... you'll tarnish the reputation of the Nameless.

t_ch: （清除「失真」）
t_en: (Clear distortion)

t_ch: 不要再说了。
t_en: Enough.

t_ch: 你不是{NICKNAME}，一点都不像。
t_en: You're not {NICKNAME}. Nothing like {M#him}{F#her}.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}也绝不会在这里咄咄逼人，劝我放弃对我而言重要的事情。
t_en: {NICKNAME} would never try and force me to give up something so important to me.

t_ch: 我不管你是谁，现在立刻消失，让出路来，不要再冒充我的朋友了。
t_en: I don't care who you are — disappear right now and let me pass. And don't you ever pretend to be one of my friends again.

t_ch: （再等等）
t_en: (Wait)

t_ch: 谢谢你出手，符玄小姐…
t_en: Thanks for your help, Miss Fu Xuan...

t_ch: 哼，我答应过要看顾你。何况我受惠于「{RUBY_B#博识尊}遍智天君{RUBY_E#}」，绝不能容忍有人设置障碍，阻挡他人探寻真相。
t_en: Hmph, I promised I'd watch over you. Besides, I've been blessed by the {RUBY_B#Nous}Wisdomwalker{RUBY_E#} — I will never tolerate efforts to impede the truth.

t_ch: 刚才那个人说自己是流光忆庭的「信使」。流光忆庭…他们为什么要阻挠我？
t_en: That person said she was a messenger. The Garden of Recollection... Why would they come after me?

t_ch: 忆庭中各怀异志的人可不少，仅凭一句自报家门没法确认其中原委。
t_en: Many members of the Garden have their own agenda — it's impossible to confirm anything from a cursory introduction.

t_ch: 这里就是星穹列车了吧？看起来，你快要回溯到自己所经历的源头了。到那时候，大衍穷观阵就可以开始演算了。
t_en: This is the Astral Express, no? It would seem that you're nearing the source. The Matrix of Prescience Ultima should start making further calculations soon.

t_ch: 你还记得自己是在什么地方被发现的吗？
t_en: Do you remember where it was exactly that you were discovered?

t_ch: 他们当时…把封住我的冰块就放在了如今我住的客厢里。
t_en: They... set the ice down in one of the passenger cars — it ended up being my room.

t_ch: 那就穿过这节列车，找到你的房间吧。
t_en: Then let's pass through this carriage and find your room.

t_ch: 怎么回事…怎么又回到起点了？
t_en: What's going on... How come we're back at the start?

t_ch: 是那个「信使」，她又在干扰你的行动了。
t_en: It's the messenger. She's interfering with your movements again.

t_ch: 我当然希望能收留她…或者说，仅凭最基本的道德判断也能得出结论——既然我们发现了她，就必须照顾好她。
t_en: Of course I hope we can take her in... Or rather, I can offer a conclusion based on the most basic of moral judgments: Now that we've found her, we have to look after her.

t_ch: 但是，我也很担心这样的选择会不会给星穹列车带来麻烦……
t_en: But naturally, I'm concerned about whether or not this places the Astral Express in any danger...

t_ch: 星穹列车是欢迎每个人的帕！不用担心，你只要做出当下最正确的选择就好，帕姆会为此后一切问题负责的。
t_en: The Astral Express welcomes everyone! Don't worry — do what you feel is right, and Pom-Pom will take care of the rest.

t_ch: 有你这句话…我大概能安心一些了。只希望她能在列车上住得顺心，不要被我们这些怪人吓到……
t_en: Thanks... I feel more at ease now. I just hope she'll have a good time on the Express and not feel too alienated from the rest of us...

t_ch: 不用担心帕！帕姆都说了，星穹列车是欢迎每个人的帕！
t_en: Stop worrying so much! Pom-Pom will say it again: The Astral Express welcomes everyone!

t_ch: 封住那个孩子的像是冰，却没有冰的物理性质…真是怪了。
t_en: It seemed like that kid was trapped in ice, but the substance doesn't share the same physical properties... How bizarre.

t_ch: 这些晶体在不同条件下，呈现出了至少六种晶相结构…「六相之冰」，银河真是一道深不可测的谜题。
t_en: The crystals show at least six structures under differing conditions... "Six-Phased Ice." The galaxy really is an enigma.

t_ch: 他们都同意收留你。我是个被放逐之人，你看起来也不像有可以归去的地方…但至少在这里，我们都不会是孤身一人了。
t_en: Everyone agreed to take you in... I'm an exile, and by the looks of things, you might not have a home to go back to, either... But here on the Express, nobody is alone.

t_ch: 姬子小姐说你的身体并无大碍，但也不知道你什么时候才会醒来……
t_en: Himeko said that your vitals were all stable... she just didn't know when you'd regain consciousness...

t_ch: 干扰你的人始终在阻挠你前进……
t_en: The interferer has been thwarting your progress this whole time...

t_ch: 也许「后退」才能找到你的过去。
t_en: Maybe the only way for you to find your past is to take a step back.

t_ch: 「不要回头，继续向前走。」我完全明白了，只要和干扰者反着来，就能找到出路。真不愧是我。
t_en: "Don't look back. Keep going forward..." I get it! If I do the exact opposite — I'll find my way out! You're a true genius, March.

t_ch: 在你打开这扇门的同时，穷观阵也会开始对你的过去进行演算。
t_en: As soon as you open this door, the Matrix of Prescience will start calculating your past.

t_ch: 准备好了的话，就开始吧。
t_en: Whenever you're ready...

t_ch: （开门）
t_en: (Open the door)

t_ch: （再等等）
t_en: (Wait)

t_ch: 既然你那么想知道自己的过往…那跟我来吧。
t_en: If you are truly set on learning about your past... then come with me.

t_ch: 好难受…符玄小姐，怎么突然就中断了推演？
t_en: Ugh... Miss Fu Xuan, why did the simulation just cut off like that?

t_ch: 你没事吧？
t_en: Are you okay?

t_ch: 我没事…就是有些难受……
t_en: I'm fine... Just a little light-headed is all...

t_ch: 发生了什么？
t_en: What happened?

t_ch: 我甚至还没搞清楚发生了什么，就被弹出来了……
t_en: I don't know. Before I could figure out what was going on, it spat me out...

t_ch: 穷观阵在刚才的那一瞬间停转了。
t_en: ...And the Matrix of Prescience stopped at precisely the same moment!

t_ch: 三月，自打你触摸那块「冰」之后，我就再也观测不到你了。究竟发生了什么？
t_en: March, I lost sight of you once you touched that block of ice. What happened?

t_ch: 还真有点不好解释……
t_en: It's kinda hard to explain...

t_ch: 三月七和符玄向你讲述了刚才发生的一切。你也向她们讲述了关于「信使」的事。
t_en: March and Fu Xuan try their best to recount everything that just happened. You also tell them about the Messenger.

t_ch: 所以…那位神秘人的确是流光天君的使者。
t_en: So... That mysterious girl was really a messenger of the Glowswather?

t_ch: 所以…是浮黎？和我的过去有关的星神是「记忆」的浮黎？
t_en: Glowswather... you mean Fuli the Remembrance? Are they the Aeon connected to my past?

t_ch: 不，还不能这么断言…只能说，很可能是一位星神封印了你的记忆，但祂未必是流光天君。
t_en: No, it's too early to draw such a conclusion... All I can say is that it was very likely an Aeon that sealed away your memories... but not necessarily Fuli.

t_ch: 也许「信使」所说的「保护」，就是在暗示流光天君并非封印你记忆的罪魁祸首。祂插手只是为了保护你不被封印的记忆所伤害……
t_en: When the messenger spoke of "protection," perhaps she meant the following: The Glowswather wasn't the one who sealed away your memories. THEY are interfering to shield you from these very memories.

t_ch: 所以到头来还是一无所获。
t_en: Looks like we came away empty-handed...

t_ch: 那岂不是什么都不知道？
t_en: Sounds like we're still none the wiser...

t_ch: 我觉得倒也不至于如此悲观啦…至少我知道了流光忆庭这条线索？
t_en: It's not so bad... At least I know it has to do with the Garden of Recollection, right?

t_ch: 看来想寻回记忆，已经超出大衍穷观阵的能力范围了。
t_en: It seems like retrieving memories is beyond the operational spectrum of the Matrix of Prescience Ultima.

t_ch: 没关系啦！太卜大人肯伸出援手，我感激不尽啦！
t_en: That's alright! You've helped me so much already, Master Diviner. I owe you a big one.

t_ch: 如果没别的事的话，我得赶紧组织人力检修一番…可恶，若是穷观阵有所损伤，我会心疼死的。
t_en: Mm... If you'll excuse me, I must organize an inspection of the Matrix... Ugh, fingers crossed there's no damage done.

t_ch: 那么咱们就先走一步了。{NICKNAME}，赶快闪人吧，我怕太卜大人查出什么损坏，要向列车寄天价维修账单哩。
t_en: We'll leave you to it then! {NICKNAME}, run — if we broke something there'll be a huge bill to pay!

t_ch: 我刚才一摸口袋，突然发现口袋里多了这么个东西。
t_en: Huh, I just found this in my pocket... How did it get there?

t_ch: 这是什么？
t_en: What is it?

t_ch: 这应该是一个光锥吧？在穷观阵里面，那个奇怪的声音不是说，要送给我一个礼物吗…看起来就是这个了吧？
t_en: Looks like a Light Cone. Didn't that weird voice in the Matrix say something about a parting gift? I guess this is it.

t_ch: 这里面封存的是我的「起点」…不知怎么，我总觉得他们没有恶意……
t_en: My "origin," huh... I don't know why, but the voice sounded friendly to me.

t_ch: 我相信小三月的直觉。
t_en: I trust your intuition.

t_ch: 是吧…所以这份礼物，我也就欣然笑纳啦！
t_en: Well, guess I'll keep it then!

t_ch: 我不好说。
t_en: I don't know about that...

t_ch: 唔…毕竟你比较谨慎嘛。但无论如何，礼物没有不收的道理，不然多没礼貌啊！
t_en: *sigh* You're too cautious. A gift is a gift — it's rude not to take it!

t_ch: 虽然在穷观阵里真是吃尽了苦头，但拿到了这个小礼物，就觉得还不算坏。
t_en: Jumping through all those hoops in the Matrix was pretty exhausting, but this little gift makes me feel like it was worth it.

t_ch: 改天还要去好好拜谢一下符玄小姐，真是多亏了她的帮助呀。
t_en: I'll have to find time to thank Miss Fu Xuan properly. I really owe her one.

t_ch: 话说，你不觉得这光锥里面的我特别可爱吗？
t_en: Hey, the "me" in the Light Cone looks kinda cute, don't you think?

t_ch: 确实。
t_en: ...Sure.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，因为本姑娘就是实诚。
t_en: Hee-hee, just telling it like I see it.

t_ch: 比你本人可爱。
t_en: Cuter than the real you.

t_ch: 你原来是口是心非那一型的啊……
t_en: Hmph, don't believe you.

t_ch: 不如你本人可爱。
t_en: Not as cute as the real you.

t_ch: 那是自然的。光锥哪能和活蹦乱跳的本人相比呢？
t_en: Obviously! How could "Light Cone me" be cuter than actual me?

t_ch: 得赶紧组织人力检修一番…可恶，若是穷观阵有所损伤，我会心疼死的。
t_en: I must organize an inspection of the Matrix... Ugh, fingers crossed there's no damage done.

t_ch: 欢迎光临「梦境贩售店」，星穹列车的开拓客！
t_en: Trailblazers from the Astral Express — Welcome to Dreamscape Sales Store!

t_ch: 你就这一句开场白吗？
t_en: Is that usually how you welcome customers?

t_ch: 请见谅，对于您这样身份尊贵的客人，我难以找到更妥帖的称呼。
t_en: Please excuse me. I struggle to think of an appropriate way of addressing such esteemed guests as yourselves.

t_ch: 好久不见，大眼仔。
t_en: Long time no see, ol' big eye.

t_ch: 好久不见，历劫佳人。
t_en: Long time no see, diamond in the rough.

t_ch: 您一定是来体验那枚梦泡的，我已做好准备。请注意，虽然鄙店的商品均已通过「<unbreak>027</unbreak>号-欠损费勒斯」和「<unbreak>076</unbreak>号-实在界荒原」两项安全协议检验……
t_en: You must be here to experience those dream bubbles. I've already made the necessary preparations. Be careful, though — we did pass the safety inspections stipulated by "Article <unbreak>027</unbreak>-Damaged Faris" and "Article <unbreak>076</unbreak>-Real Wasteland..."

t_ch: 但这场梦…它是一段尚未经过修饰和打磨的新鲜记忆，生猛又刺激。
t_en: ...However, this dreamscape will feature unfiltered and unpolished fresh memories. It will make for a very vivid experience.

t_ch: 资深玩家将这种梦称为「刺身」，我不确定这点是否合您胃口。如有任何不适，请立即离开梦境，并寻求专业的医疗帮助。
t_en: Veteran gamers call this type of dream a "thrill-dream" — I don't know if you're into that kind of thing? If you feel any discomfort, please leave the dreamscape immediately and seek professional medical help.

t_ch: ——很幸运，我正是一位优秀的医生，虽然是精神科的，哈哈！让我们开始吧。请您闭上眼睛，将额头抵在梦泡上。
t_en: ...Luckily, I just so happen to be an excellent doctor, albeit a psychiatric one, haha! Let us begin. Please close your eyes, and rest your forehead on the dream bubble.

t_ch: （触碰梦泡）
t_en: (Touch the dream bubble)

t_ch: 对了，那位优雅的女士临行前还有一句赠言——
t_en: Oh yes, I nearly forgot — that elegant girl mentioned something before she left—

t_ch: 「这段记忆的性质有些特殊，为了其他顾客的梦境体验，还请不要向任何人透露它的结局。」
t_en: "The nature of this memory is special. Don't ruin the dreamscape experience for anyone else by spoiling the ending."

t_ch: 还是有点不放心…
t_en: I'm not so sure about this...

t_ch: 如果您需要时间准备，鄙店愿意无限期等待。
t_en: If you need time to prepare, we can humbly wait as long as you need.

t_ch: 准备好体验黑天鹅女士的美梦了吗？——请您闭上眼睛，将额头抵在梦泡上。
t_en: Are you ready to experience Miss Black Swan's dreamscape? Please close your eyes, and rest your forehead on the dream bubble.

t_ch: （触碰梦泡）
t_en: (Touch the dream bubble)

t_ch: 再等一下…
t_en: Hang on a second...

t_ch: 如果您需要时间准备，鄙店愿意无限期等待。
t_en: If you need time to prepare, we can humbly wait as long as you need.

t_ch: 黑天鹅女士的梦泡到此为止了。请问您对这次体验还满意吗？
t_en: That's the end of Miss Black Swan's dream bubble. Are you satisfied with your experience?

t_ch: 好有意思。
t_en: How fascinating.

t_ch: 我也认为这段美梦相当有趣。您在其中收获了快乐，黑天鹅女士也一定会感到欣慰吧。
t_en: I also found this dream suitably fascinating. You've gained joy from it. And Miss Black Swan surely also feels gratified, yes?

t_ch: 如果下次有缘再见，我会向她转达您的好评。
t_en: If we meet again, I will pass your esteemed praise on to her.

t_ch: 好没意思。
t_en: How boring.

t_ch: 我理解。梦境本就是十人十色，注定不会适合所有人。
t_en: I understand. The dreamscape is inherently different for everyone, so inevitably there are some people who it won't suit.

t_ch: 但我想，黑天鹅女士留下这枚梦泡定有她的用意。不过，这就不是我一介局外人能妄断的了。
t_en: But, I also think Miss Black Swan must have her own reason for holding on to this dream bubble. And as an outsider, that is not my place to judge.

t_ch: 有点好猜。
t_en: It was kinda easy.

t_ch: 的确，那些谜题对于您这样经验丰富的开拓者而言，是有些简单了。
t_en: Sure enough, those puzzles are going to be easy for a Trailblazer with the vast experience that you have.

t_ch: 不过恐怕那位花火小姐又会辩称：「我们是『假面愚者』，又不是『假面天才』！」…哈哈。
t_en: But I'm afraid that Miss Sparkle would argue: "We're Masked Fools, not Masked Geniuses!" ...haha.

t_ch: 桑博竟然是…
t_en: Sampo turned out to be...

t_ch: 我注意到，这段梦里似乎出现了您熟悉的人。需要再次提醒，梦只是梦，在匹诺康尼，梦可以被加工，也可以被篡改。
t_en: I noticed that the people in your dream were familiar to you. It's worth repeating that dreams are just dreams, and in Penacony dreams can be processed or even tampered with.

t_ch: 梦中之事不可尽信，更何况是他人的梦境。如有任何想要确认的信息…还是在清醒的现实中完成吧。祝您做个好梦。
t_en: Things in our dreams cannot be trusted, let alone in someone else's dreamscape. If there's any information that you'd like to confirm... please do so by the cold hard light of reality. Sweet dreams.

t_ch: 这可真是一场特别的美梦。您注意到它嵌套式的结构了吗？在梦境中参与出演一场舞台剧，就像梦中梦一样诡异又迷人。
t_en: What a special dream this has been. Did you notice its nested structure? Being part of a play inside the dreamscape is as weird and captivating as being in a dream within a dream.

t_ch: 能再和我讲讲黑天鹅的梦泡吗？
t_en: Can tell me more about Black Swan's dream bubble?

t_ch: （自从三月拜了彦卿和云璃两位师父学剑，列车里都听不见她的声音了……）
t_en: (Ever since March started learning swordplay from Yanqing and Yunli, her voice has been absent on the Express...)

t_ch: （也不知她学得怎么样了，怎么连个人影都见不着？）
t_en: (I wonder how well she's progressing. Where has she been these days?)

t_ch: 你来了。
t_en: Oh, there you are.

t_ch: 你没来的时候，我已经逛了…找了一圈，三月七不在这儿。
t_en: I was looking around before you got here... I already looked everywhere, but March 7th is nowhere to be found.

t_ch: 不用找三月七了？
t_en: So, no need to keep looking for March 7th?

t_ch: 不用找了，就当她是逃课成功好了。
t_en: Nope, let's just assume she successfully skipped class.

t_ch: 也许三月七就在…
t_en: Maybe March 7th is actually...

t_ch: 算了吧，就当她是逃课成功好了。
t_en: Forget it, let's just assume she successfully skipped class.

t_ch: 三月七练剑算是勤快，这些日子也没怎么休息，确实应该给她放一天假的。
t_en: Actually, she's been quite diligent in her swordplay practice and hasn't taken many breaks lately. I think she deserves a day off.

t_ch: 再说了，我过去练剑的时候比她更会逃课——有了兴致，我能练个三天三夜；没有兴致，师兄师姐谁也找不着我。
t_en: Besides, I used to skip classes even more than her back when I was still learning. If I was in the mood, I could train for three days straight, but if I wasn't in the mood, none of my seniors could find me.

t_ch: 所以，我也算是能理解她吧。
t_en: So, I can understand where she's coming from.

t_ch: 那我…是不是可以回去了？
t_en: So then... can I go back?

t_ch: 不用回去，我叫你来就绝不会亏待你。
t_en: No need. I asked for you to come, so I won't send you away empty-handed.

t_ch: 看来三月这都是跟师父学的。
t_en: Looks like she learned it from her master.

t_ch: 你就别替她找借口了。
t_en: Don't make excuses for her.

t_ch: 既然来都来了，我请你去吃点好吃的吧。
t_en: Since you're here, let me treat you to some tasty food.

t_ch: 今天，我对你更有兴趣一些。
t_en: I'm more interested in you today.

t_ch: 我？哪方面的兴趣？
t_en: Me? Interested in what?

t_ch: 先说好，我不拜师。
t_en: Let me be clear, I'm not learning swordplay from you.

t_ch: 边走边聊吧。
t_en: Let's walk and talk.

t_ch: 听灵砂姐姐说金人巷里有好吃的，走吧走吧。
t_en: Lingsha told me there's tasty food in Aurum Alley, let's go there.

t_ch: 老板，两位！
t_en: A table for two please!

t_ch: 好嘞，两位里边请，想吃点什么呢？
t_en: Alright, come on in. What would you like?

t_ch: 老板，这菜单从上到下每一样各点一份吧。
t_en: Well, we'll have one of everything on the menu.

t_ch: 这…菜单从上到下？
t_en: The... entire menu?

t_ch: 怎么，你觉得够吗？
t_en: Hmm? Do you think it'll be enough?

t_ch: 她是担心咱们俩吃不下…
t_en: She's worried we won't be able to finish it all...

t_ch: 别担心，我们有两个人。
t_en: Don't worry, there are two of us.

t_ch: 云璃自信地看向了你。
t_en: Yunli looks at you confidently.

t_ch: 我一天也就五十块零花钱。
t_en: I don't have much money on me right now.

t_ch: 别担心，我带钱了。
t_en: Don't worry, I've got enough money with me.

t_ch: 云璃自信地看向了你。
t_en: Yunli looks at you confidently.

t_ch: …那、那这样吧，我先去做几道菜你们先吃着，不够再加。
t_en: Well... then I'll start making a few dishes, and if you need more, just let me know.

t_ch: 谢谢老板。
t_en: Thank you.

t_ch: 真的不用再找找三月？
t_en: Are you sure we don't need to look for March?

t_ch: 不用，我考虑过了，今天就放一天假吧。
t_en: No, I've thought about it. She deserves a day off.

t_ch: …你平时吃得很多吗？
t_en: ...Do you usually eat a lot?

t_ch: 多…咳，其实也不太多。
t_en: Yeah... Ahem, I mean, not too much, actually.

t_ch: 爷爷说了，在外结交朋友，花钱绝不能手软。所以你的胃口怎么样？
t_en: Grandpa said to never be stingy when it comes to making friends. So, how big is your appetite?

t_ch: 我是小鸟胃，吃不了多少。
t_en: I don't eat much.

t_ch: 爷爷说了，他可以吃。
t_en: Then we can bring the leftovers to Grandpa.

t_ch: 我是大胃王，非常能吃！
t_en: My stomach is a bottomless pit!

t_ch: 那你多吃一点！
t_en: Then eat to your heart's content!

t_ch: 你刚才说，对我感兴趣？
t_en: Did you just say you're interested in me?

t_ch: 准确来说，我对你的剑很感兴趣。
t_en: Well, to be more precise, I'm interested in your sword.

t_ch: 我听彦卿聊起过你，他说你的实力不可小觑，武器形制也很别具一格。
t_en: Yanqing mentioned you before. He said that you're not to be underestimated and that your weapon has a unique shape.

t_ch: 所以，我一直对你的剑很感兴趣：重量分布是否均匀，用什么材质铸成，嵌入何种机巧，又以什么样的剑法运使……
t_en: So, I've always been curious about your sword — how the weight is distributed, what material it's made of, whether it contains any special ingenia, and what kind of sword techniques you use...

t_ch: 可是我用的不是剑，是球棒。
t_en: It's not a sword. It's a baseball bat.

t_ch: …字面意义上的球棒？
t_en: ...Seriously? A bat?

t_ch: 还好奇吗？
t_en: Are you still curious?

t_ch: 好奇心更强烈了。
t_en: Yeah, now more than ever.

t_ch: 让你失望了？
t_en: Are you disappointed?

t_ch: 恰恰相反，这很有趣！
t_en: Just the opposite — this is fascinating!

t_ch: 好啊，也不是稀罕东西。
t_en: Alright, alright, it's not anything special.

t_ch: 你将球棒，你最信赖的伙伴——也许仅次于三月七、丹恒、瓦尔特、姬子和帕姆的伙伴，放在云璃面前。
t_en: You place your beloved bat, your most trusted companion — perhaps second only to the likes of March 7th, Dan Heng, Welt, Himeko, and Pom-Pom — before Yunli's gaze.

t_ch: 还真是一根球棒。
t_en: So, it really is a baseball bat.

t_ch: 爷爷说了，剑无定型。你这球棒倒也算是一种「剑」。不过爷爷还说了，物肖主人形。你知道吗？你的武器会反映出你的习惯和性格。
t_en: Grandpa said that a sword doesn't have to be constrained by its shape, so in a sense, your bat is a sword too. He also said that a weapon mimics its master, so your bat actually reflects your habits and nature, you know?

t_ch: 让我猜猜，你是一个…不遵循常规的人，喜欢即兴发挥，面对真正的强敌也能从容应对，我可有说错？
t_en: Let me guess, you're someone who... doesn't follow the rules, enjoys improvising, and can handle yourself well against tough foes. Am I right?

t_ch: 你在我身后装摄像头了吗？
t_en: Have you been secretly filming me?

t_ch: 准吧？我猜你平时还总说「规则就是用来打破的」之类的怪话。
t_en: Amazing, right? And I bet you always say something silly like "Rules are made to be broken."

t_ch: 你说错了，我非常遵守规则。
t_en: Oh, you're mistaken. I always follow the rules.

t_ch: 是吗？我一向是不会看走眼的。
t_en: Oh, really? But I never guess wrong.

t_ch: 我曾经还见过不少道貌岸然的剑主，说起谎来就像是吃饭喝水一般自然。不过剑总是诚实的——剑会告诉我，它的主人不过是「附庸于神兵利器」的宵小之辈。
t_en: I've come across so many sword wielders who acted all righteous and moral but were really just liars. Their swords always told the truth though. They told me that their wielders were just a bunch of frauds who relied on their divine weapons for everything.

t_ch: 迄今为止，我猎获数百柄剑皆是如此。这些剑会被逐一熔断，绝无例外！
t_en: I've hunted down hundreds of swords like this so far, and every single one of them has been melted down!

t_ch: 你会抢走别人的剑并熔断它？
t_en: Wait, you take people's swords by force and melt them down?

t_ch: 我只对那些失格之人发起挑战，而熔炼是我保护那些剑器的方式。
t_en: Yes, but only for those who cross the line. Melting their swords is my way of protecting those weapons.

t_ch: 就没有误判、错判的时候吗？
t_en: But what if you make a mistake?

t_ch: 剑比语言更为真诚。比起判断一百句话，判断一柄剑更容易，所以我很少犯错。
t_en: Swords are more honest than words. It's much easier to judge a sword than to judge someone's words, so I rarely make mistakes.

t_ch: 说回来，光是观察剑还是不够的——爷爷说过，言语能欺瞒人，表情能欺瞒人，在生死相搏中，剑招从不欺骗人…演武仪典近在眼前，你会上台的对吧？对吧？
t_en: But just looking at a sword is not enough. Grandpa always says, words and expressions can be deceiving, but in a fight to the death, a sword's movements never lie... The Wardance is just around the corner. You'll be participating, right?

t_ch: 云璃小姐，对善战之人来说，演武仪典确实是个一展身手，博取名声的好机会。
t_en: Miss Yunli, for talented swordmasters like you, the Wardance is a perfect opportunity to show off your skills and make a name for yourself.

t_ch: 不过像{NICKNAME}这样早已为仙舟立下赫赫功劳的老朋友，怕是没必要在演武台上出风头。
t_en: But for people like {NICKNAME} who have already made significant contributions to the Xianzhou, they maybe don't need to prove themselves on the Wardance stage.

t_ch: 好久不见了，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: It has been a while, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: …你是？
t_en: ...And you are?

t_ch: …你是？
t_en: ...And you are?

t_ch: 两位真是贵人多忘事。自我介绍一下，小女子夕葵，是驭空司舵的秘书，之一。
t_en: Ah, I supposed busy people have short memories. Please let me introduce myself. I'm Xikui, one of the secretaries serving the Helm Master Yukong.

t_ch: 这位是驭空大人的秘书。
t_en: This is Madam Yukong's secretary.

t_ch: 感谢{NICKNAME}介绍，小女子夕葵在司辰宫担任司舵秘书。
t_en: Thank you for your introduction, {NICKNAME}. I'm Xikui, and I work in the Palace of Astrum as the secretary for the Helm Master.

t_ch: 唐突打断二位实在抱歉。只是刚巧听两位谈起演武仪典，{NICKNAME}又是老熟人了，我才过来打个招呼。
t_en: I apologize for interrupting your conversation. I happened to hear you discussing the Wardance, and since {NICKNAME} is an old acquaintance, I thought I'd come and say hello.

t_ch: 夕葵小姐在招待客人？
t_en: Are you hosting any guests, Miss Xikui?

t_ch: 演武仪典召开，罗浮上来了不少异邦外域的客人。像我身边这位，是来自遥远星辰卡勒瓦拉的客人，帕沃尔先生。
t_en: Yes, with the Wardance approaching, many guests from afar are pouring in. Mr. Paavo here is one of them. He's from the distant planet Kalevala.

t_ch: 帕沃尔先生的故乡才受邀加入泛银河贸易体系不久，这一次他带着使团前来仙舟做客，不仅为了生意，也是为了归还属于仙舟的东西。
t_en: Mr. Paavo's planet recently joined the Pan-Cosmic Trade System. He brought his delegation here this time not only for business but also to return something that belongs to the Xianzhou.

t_ch: 夕葵小姐，说是「归还」，不如说「送回」更恰当。虽然它属于仙舟，但咱们毕竟千里迢迢，费了不少周折才把礼物送到。
t_en: Miss Xikui, the word "gift" is perhaps more fitting than "return." While it did once belong to the Xianzhou, we faced many tribulations to be able to deliver it here, so it should be considered a gift.

t_ch: 抱歉抱歉，是我的措辞不当。帕沃尔先生的使团送回了一柄流落异乡多年的仙舟宝剑。
t_en: I apologize for my poor choice of words. Mr. Paavo's delegation brought a legendary Xianzhou sword that had been lost for centuries.

t_ch: 怀炎将军打算在司辰宫亲自致谢，将宝剑作为罗浮演武仪典胜者的奖品赠出。我和帕沃尔先生正打算前往那儿。
t_en: General Huaiyan plans to personally thank them at the Palace of Astrum and present the sword as a prize to the champion of the Wardance. Mr. Paavo and I were actually on our way there.

t_ch: 听两位兴致勃勃地聊着演武仪典，不知道你们有没有兴趣一同顺道去司辰宫看看热闹？
t_en: Since you two were talking so enthusiastically about the Wardance, maybe you'd be interested in joining us at Palace of Astrum?

t_ch: 两位好啊。来罗浮仙舟一趟虽然不容易，但这儿的光景确实和故乡大不一样，真是不虚此行。
t_en: Nice to meet you both. The journey to the Xianzhou Luofu wasn't an easy one, but the scenery here is worth it. It's completely different from my homeland.

t_ch: 等司辰宫的赠剑手续完成，我会在这条街道挑选一处清静的地方，栽下幼苗，届时我的任务也就完成了。
t_en: Once the formalities at the Palace of Astrum are finished, I'll find a quiet spot on this street and plant a seedling, and then my mission will be complete.

t_ch: 英雄之剑物归原主，可喜可贺，可喜可贺！
t_en: This sword of heroes will finally be returned to its rightful owners! I couldn't be happier.

t_ch: 聊聊卡勒瓦拉。
t_en: Let's talk about Kalevala.

t_ch: 你对鄙人的家乡感兴趣？呵呵，那敢情好，欢迎阁下有空来咱们那儿做客。
t_en: Oh, you're interested in my home planet? That's great! Your Excellency is welcome to visit us at any time.

t_ch: 虽然贸易航线刚通，从庇尔波因特出发，大概需要两百个系统时的跃迁才能抵达。不过咱们那儿真的很不错！
t_en: Even though the trade route just opened, it still might take around two hundred system hours to get there from Pier Point, but our planet is really great!

t_ch: 就是平日里冷了些，饮食单调了些，房子也没庇尔波因特那么高。但是不打紧！咱们那儿发现了储量巨大的超钛矿藏，要不了多久就会繁荣起来的！
t_en: Sure, it's a bit cold, and the food is a bit repetitive, and our houses aren't as fancy as those on Pier Point, but that doesn't matter! We've discovered massive supernium deposits, and we're on the path to prosperity!

t_ch: 聊聊宝剑。
t_en: Let's talk about the sword.

t_ch: 喔，你说「米卡•奇瓦沙」啊！那可是把不得了的剑。它在我的故乡有着崇高的地位，曾经帮助神话英雄斩妖除魔。
t_en: Oh, you mean the Miekka Kivessä! It's an incredible sword. That weapon helped the legendary heroes of my homeland slay demons and is highly revered.

t_ch: 同时它也是选拔君主的剑，据说卡勒瓦拉的先民国王们都曾得到这把宝剑的回应，因而拥有了称王的资格。
t_en: It also has the power to choose monarchs. All the ancient kings of Kalevala received a sign from the sword, showing they were worthy to rule the land.

t_ch: 按照传统，历任国王会将剑刺入石碑，只有受剑认可的英雄才能拔出它，继任王位。
t_en: According to tradition, each monarch would plunge the sword into a stone, and only a hero recognized by the sword could pull it out and ascend to the throne next.

t_ch: 这是什么生物识别技术？
t_en: Is it some sort of biometric technology?

t_ch: 这把宝剑还能选贤任能？
t_en: That sword can actually appoint worthy monarchs?

t_ch: 这都是过去的传说，现在谁也没见过。
t_en: Well, those are just legends from the past. No one today has witnessed it firsthand.

t_ch: 为什么不留下宝剑？
t_en: Why don't you keep the sword for yourselves?

t_ch: 一来嘛，时代变了，议会成立，「拔剑成王」的神话早就是个笑话了；二来，<color=#dbc291ff>拔出这柄剑，以及辨认出它仙舟来历的，并不是卡勒瓦拉人，而是一位天外来客。</color>
t_en: Well, times have changed, and now that we have a council in place, the idea of a sword crowning a king has become a joke. Besides, <color=#dbc291ff>it wasn't a native of Kalevala who last pulled out the sword and recognized its connection to the Xianzhou ship — it was a visitor from afar.</color>

t_ch: 虽然对卡勒瓦拉而言它是件颇有历史价值的古物，但毕竟它不属于这个世界。
t_en: While the sword holds great historical significance for Kalevala, it doesn't truly belong to our world.

t_ch: 关于要不要把剑送回，议会里的老东西们吵了一架，却难得有了一致的意见：让「米卡•奇瓦沙」回到它该去的地方。
t_en: The old timers on council had a heated debate about whether to return the sword. They eventually unanimously agreed to let the Miekka Kivessä return to where it truly belongs.

t_ch: 没有比这更好的安排了！
t_en: That's the best possible outcome.

t_ch: 我打算去一同见证。
t_en: I'm going to witness its return, too.

t_ch: 好极了，能有这么多人见证宝剑归还，真是荣幸。
t_en: Wonderful! It's an honor to have so many people witness the sword's homecoming.

t_ch: 好极了，有这么多人见证宝剑归还，真是荣幸。
t_en: Wonderful! It's an honor to have so many people witness the sword's homecoming.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，一起去司辰宫瞧瞧？
t_en: Let's head to the Palace of Astrum together, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 我对这柄剑没兴趣。
t_en: I'm not interested in the sword, to be honest.

t_ch: 可是我很感兴趣，毕竟那可是演武仪典的奖品！
t_en: But I am! It's the prize for the Wardance, after all!

t_ch: 虽然我不懂那些真真假假的传说故事，但品鉴真正的宝剑，只需一眼我就能明白。
t_en: I might not know all the stories and legends, but I know a legendary sword when I see one.

t_ch: 我也想瞧瞧这柄剑。
t_en: I'd love to take a look at that sword too.

t_ch: 对吧对吧，还真得感谢三月七逃课，咱俩才有机会能凑上这场热闹。
t_en: I know, right? Thanks to March 7th skipping class, we get to join in on the fun.

t_ch: 爷爷平日总会叫上我一起赏剑。今天这番场合却没向我提过一个字。真是奇怪。
t_en: Grandpa always asks me to go look at swords with him, but he hasn't said anything about this one. It's really weird.

t_ch: 不过既然有人邀请，那是最好不过了。{NICKNAME}，走吧，我们一起去瞧瞧。
t_en: But now that we're invited, let's go and check it out, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 再过一会儿，怀炎将军就会在司辰宫中举行赠剑仪式。{NICKNAME}不去瞧瞧吗？
t_en: General Huaiyan will hold the sword-gifting ceremony at the Palace of Astrum soon. Are you interested, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 你来了，走吧，一起去赠剑仪式！
t_en: There you are! Come on, let's go to the ceremony together!

t_ch: 走吧。
t_en: Let's go.

t_ch: 现在还有别的事情。
t_en: I've got something else going on.

t_ch: 在仪式开始前，请允许我感谢骑士银枝。感谢他让我们得知宝剑的来历，也感谢他一路护送，令我等不辱使命，平安抵达。
t_en: Before the ceremony officially commences, I want to express my gratitude to the Knight Argenti. Thank you for enlightening us about the sword's origin and for escorting it here safely so that we may complete this ceremony.

t_ch: 我，帕沃尔•卡拉萨加，谨代表卡勒瓦拉使团，将宝剑「米卡•奇瓦沙」交还仙舟联盟，它本来自仙舟，也应当归于故乡。
t_en: On behalf of the Kalevalan Delegation, I, Paavo Kalastaja, am here to return the legendary Miekka Kivessä to the Xianzhou Alliance, its rightful homeland.

t_ch: 感谢卡勒瓦拉的还剑义举，也感谢纯美骑士的侠肝义胆，怀炎在此借罗浮宝地，正式奉迎宝剑归乡。
t_en: I'm truly grateful for Kalevala's noble act of returning the sword and for the Knight of Beauty's chivalry. Now, as we gather here on the Xianzhou Luofu, I officially welcome the sword back to its homeland.

t_ch: 「米卡•奇瓦沙」，是个不错的名字，想必与它所经历的一切有关。
t_en: Miekka Kivessä. It's a fitting name for a sword with such a legendary past.

t_ch: 但我依旧还记得这把剑自出炉之日起所镌刻的字铭，孤云。它出自朱明仙舟焰轮铸炼宫的匠人「含光」之手。
t_en: I still remember the name engraved on this sword the day it was forged, Guyun. It was forged by Hanguang, a craftsman from the Zhuming's Flamewheel Forge.

t_ch: 等等…含、含光？
t_en: Wait... H—Hanguang?

t_ch: 虽然神锋蒙尘、遍布裂纹，但此剑筋骨正如仙舟云骑，历战不折。只需觅工造司良工修缮打磨，便能令它重焕旧日神采。
t_en: Although its blade is worn and cracked, its essence remains as resilient as the Xianzhou Cloud Knights. All it needs is some repairs and polishing by the skilled craftsmen of the Artisanship Commission to regain its former glory.

t_ch: 老朽决定将这柄孤云托付给罗浮工造司修复，作为本次罗浮演武仪典守擂胜者的奖品。它将被用于斩杀孽物、卫蔽家园，如此便不负卡勒瓦拉使团迢迢还剑之情。
t_en: I've decided to entrust this sword, Guyun, to the Luofu Artisanship Commission for restoration. Furthermore, it will serve as the prize for the champion of the Luofu Wardance. This sword will be wielded against abominations, to protect our homeland, and live up to the mission of the Kalevalan delegation that returned it from so far away.

t_ch: 感谢将军，卡勒瓦拉不胜荣幸。
t_en: Thank you, General. It is an honor for Kalevala.

t_ch: 请这位骑士和使团成员一同，前往工造司，在那儿——
t_en: Now, esteemed Knight and delegation members, please head to the Artisanship Commission, where —

t_ch: 不行，这把剑可是含光……
t_en: No! This sword was forged by Hanguang...

t_ch: 云璃！这位客人将你父亲的遗作带了回来，还不快快致谢！
t_en: Yunli! These esteemed guests have brought back your father's work. Where are your manners!?

t_ch: 没想到这把剑竟然出自令尊，能将它物归原主，真是——
t_en: I didn't realize this sword was actually forged by your father. Returning it to its rightful owner is —

t_ch: 爷爷，现在就把那柄魔剑给我，我要熔了那人留下的祸根！
t_en: Grandpa! Give me that cursed sword right now! I'll melt away the remnants of that man's curse once and for all!

t_ch: （云璃，注意场合！）
t_en: (Calm down, Yunli!)

t_ch: （魔…魔剑？）
t_en: (A c—cursed sword?)

t_ch: （也许我不该有心理活动…）
t_en: (Maybe I shouldn't react...)

t_ch: 云璃，不得无礼！快向远道而来的客人道歉！
t_en: Don't be rude, Yunli! Apologize to our esteemed guests from afar!

t_ch: 对不起！
t_en: I'm sorry!

t_ch: 但是对不起归对不起，我还是会亲手熔断…这柄魔剑！我绝不会让它落在任何人手里！
t_en: I'm sorry for making a scene, but I have to melt down this cursed sword! I won't let it fall into anyone else's hands!

t_ch: 云璃，你先出去。
t_en: Yunli, please go for now.

t_ch: 就是考虑到这样的情况才瞒着她。本不欲节外生枝，却反倒弄巧成拙，冥冥中莫非真有天意？
t_en: This is exactly why I didn't want Yunli to know about this. I didn't want to create trouble, but trouble found its way here anyway. Perhaps it really is divine intervention?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，能麻烦你跟着云璃吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, could you please keep an eye on Yunli for me?

t_ch: 交给我吧，不会让她惹事的。
t_en: Sure thing. I'll make sure she doesn't get into any trouble.

t_ch: 那可有点难，拜托你了。
t_en: That is a tall order, but I'm counting on you.

t_ch: 将军的家务事，让我去不好吧？
t_en: General, is it really my place to get involved in your family matters?

t_ch: 哪有什么不好的，麻烦你了。
t_en: Don't worry about it. I need your help on this.
